title: Haber Forms and Greek-Origin Nouns
description: Learn the forms of 'haber' for past constructions and the gender of Greek-origin nouns ending in -ma.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Let's review the forms of 'haber' used to form the past. Give me 'I have' and 'we have'.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. Now give me the rest of the forms.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. Nouns ending in 'a' are usually feminine, like 'la casa'. But nouns ending in 'ma' are masculine, like 'el problema'.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: How would you say 'Have you understood the problem?' using 'comprender' and speaking formally?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. 'Ha entendido el problema'. Words ending in 'ma' like 'problema' are masculine because they come from Greek. How would you say 'the system'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: El sistema. How would you say 'Have you understood the system?' informally?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. 'Has entendido el sistema'. 'Sentir' means 'to feel'. How would you say 'Have you felt the aroma?'

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. 'Has sentido el aroma'. Remember, words like 'el programa', 'el aroma', 'el panorama', and 'el clima' are masculine. How would you say 'Have you understood the program?'

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. How do you say 'to lose'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Perder. How would you say 'I have lost'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. To emphasize, you can say 'Yo he perdido'. How would you say 'I have got lost'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. 'Me he perdido'. To emphasize, you can say 'Yo me he perdido'. How would you say 'You have lost' informally?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. 'Has perdido'. To emphasize, say 'Tú has perdido'. How would you say 'You have got lost'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. 'Te has perdido'. To emphasize, say 'Tú te has perdido'. What is 'we have' from 'haber'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Hemos. How would you say 'We have lost it'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. 'Lo hemos perdido'. How would you say 'We have lost ourselves'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. 'Nos hemos perdido'. To emphasize, say 'Nosotros nos hemos perdido'.

outro.text: You've learned the forms of 'haber' for past constructions and how to identify masculine nouns ending in -ma.
Try using 'haber' with different verbs and practice identifying masculine nouns ending in -ma.
Understanding these patterns will help you avoid common gender mistakes in Spanish.
